highlights.summary: The Allnighters gutted out a wild, Twilight Zone type of night, 11-4 win over Holden Station on Tuesday night at Marrs Park in a game that had more plot twists than an episode of Stranger Things Season 4. For the second straight game, (Loverboy) BTro was nowhere to be found, leaving the lineup without its big gun yet again. Before the first pitch was even thrown, the pre-game chaos began when the umpires from the prior game had no idea another game was scheduled, leaving the Nighters briefly convinced they had no umps. But in a twist of divine comedy, because the team had complained about one of the umps the week prior, the league sent in a brand new crew just for the Allnighters - so the boys cheerfully waved goodbye to the old ump as he walked off. The new crew, however, brought their own brand of Twilight Zone energy, incorrectly calling three outfield plays as catches in what could only be described as a collective hallucination, and Tommy (Dembo) Dembowski was flat out robbed of what would have been an ESPN SportsCenter catch of the year. Adding to the pre-game circus, Eric (E) Moody overslept at 7pm somehow convinced the game started at 9, forcing Coach Baz to suit up at third base. Baz held his own out there making some solid plays, but the nearly 40-year-old glove he was forced to use - borrowed from (P) Parello, because of course it was - literally disintegrated on a hard liner, sending the ball rolling free and gifting Holden Station two unearned runs that Mike (The OG) Russo certainly did not deserve. Russo was nothing short of stellar on the mound all night, pitching a gutsy complete game and single-handedly keeping the Allnighters in it despite a parade of errors and unearned runs behind him. Joe Z was the offensive engine going a perfect 4-for-4 with a double and three RBI, while Pat (Hoz) Hosman turned what looked like a routine fly ball into a two-run blast over the fence that had everyone doing a double take. Darren (Dermbo) McDermott was back in classic form going 3-for-4 with three RBI looking every bit the stellar shortstop the team needs him to be. Dembo Dembowski got his bat going again from the leadoff spot driving in two and helping the top of the lineup click. Jason (Farrell) Farrell looked like his old self again crossing the plate three times, and Tyler (Thomas) Evans pulled out a nice double to keep things moving. (P) Parello got his back going, well slightly, with one hit. Assad (DaaBoo) Daaboul made some great contact showing flashes of the hitter everyone knows he can be, though he did strike out again because of course he did. Eric finally woke up and made the trek to the field, showed a nice glove at third but needed a little warm-up time to get his throws down and stop playing stare-down with baserunners. The key play of the entire game came in the final inning when Anthony (Ant) Gagliano made a heads-up fielding play at second and gunned down the lead runner at the plate - and then their cleanup hitter flew out to end the threat. The cavalry arrived late when Brendan (BMac) McCarthy came rushing straight from his kid's game to pinch hit and delivered exactly when the team needed it most, drawing a walk and ripping a hit to help spark the late rally that sealed the deal. A gutsy, scrappy, chaotic, only-the-Allnighters kind of win. Go Nighters!

The Allnighters dropped a tough one to the Paddy Kellys 11-5 on Wednesday night at Marrs Park, but honestly the scoreboard was the least bizarre thing about the evening. Before the first pitch was even thrown, Coach Baz forgot the scorebook (how do you coach without the book Baz? Looks like you forgot your bat too for this game), Dermbo McDermott was spotted trying to grab a taco while the entire team stood on the diamond staring at the parking lot waiting for him to show up, and Farrell had to leave early after his allergies escalated to what witnesses described as full cardiac arrest. Then Mike (The Stoic) Stoico somehow managed to break his finger on what should have been a routine fly ball - which he dropped of course. Rod Serling himself could not have written a stranger opening act. With all that pre-game chaos it is a miracle the Nighters even laced up their cleats let alone scored 5. The Allnighters were also missing their big gun Brendan (BTro) Trohon who chose love over ball this day and would have provided some much needed pop in a lineup that desperately needed it. As if the night needed any more Twilight Zone energy, the infamous OBI curse - One Bad Inning - made its grand return, as the Nighters gifted the Kellys a crooked number through a series of errors that would make a little league coach weep. And to add true insult to injury, after the other team launched a bomb homerun, Thomas thought it would be a great time to fire Russo a fresh ball - drilling him square in the back of the head. Nothing says team chemistry like a concussion from your own guy. Meanwhile the Kellys showed up with a ringer who apparently moonlights as a human trebuchet, launching not one but TWO bombs into the neighboring park. And if that was not enough, this same hired gun had the audacity to reach over the fence and rob Pat Hosman of what would have been a legitimate homerun - plucking it right off the top of the wall. On the bright side Anthony (Ant) Gagliano and Mike (The OG) Russo both went 2-for-2 at the plate, making everyone question why these two are not hitting at the top of the order - especially when two other Nighters struck out, which as any slow pitch player knows, should be physically impossible. Coach himself made a highlight reel catch on a fly ball that required him to run what felt like a full mile, which for a man whose cardio routine consists of walking to the fridge is frankly heroic. And through it all Russo was the only reason the score was not uglier, holding the Kellys to 5 earned runs over 7 innings despite the circus unfolding behind him. The Kellys may have doubled their payroll and brought in ringers but the Nighters are not sweating it. Everyone needs to hit the cages this week (or at minimum the Cab) and the Allnighters will be back in first place where they belong. Go Nighters!

Allnighters walked past the Wolfpack 11-4 at Marrs Park on Monday in a dominant home performance. The Nighters jumped out early with two runs in the first inning aided by excessive Wolfpack walks, and never looked back. Joe Z led the offense going 3-for-4 with a double and driving in two, while Eric (E) Moody went 2-for-2. Darren (Dermbo) McDermott, Jason Farrell, Coach Baz, and Mike Russo each chipped in with key hits. A positive sign on the night was very few runners left on base and most outs were hard-hit balls. Mike (The OG) Russo was dominant on the mound again, going all 7 innings allowing just 4 runs on 10 hits. Even a long bomb by the Wolfpack in the 7th did not rattle Russo or the Nighters. The defense, especially the golden outfield crew, made some clutch catches in the middle innings to truly deliver a heart punch to the Pack. Next up - the newly stacked Paddy Kelleys who appear to have doubled their payroll in one last push for glory. The Nighters will need to bring the juice!

Allnighters could not hold onto the lead against Pocket 9s on Wednesday at Raddin Park and fell 5-4 despite leading by three going into the bottom of the 5th. Robert (The Godfather) Barrows was the offensive highlight for AllNighters going 3 for 3 with three line shots to the right side, while Patrick Hosman, Brendan (Btro) Trohon, and Ryan Overberg each added two hits. A single by Overberg put AllNighters on the board in the top of the second and then the crew rallied in the 4th. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Unfortunately they left 11 men on base, hit into two double plays, and stranded the bases loaded in the top of the 7th. Barrows, Overberg, and Joe Zametjis each drove in one run. Pocket 9s finished things off with a walk-off single RBI in the bottom of the 7th. If the Allnighters look to repeat this year, they better get their bats a little warmer.

Allnighters defeated Franchise 9-5 on Monday. The Nighters jumped out to the lead in the bottom of the first inning after Brendan (Btro) Trohon singled scoring one run, Joe Zametjis homered scoring three runs, and Darren (Dermbo) McDermott singled scoring one run. A single by Brendan (Bmac) McCarthy, a single by Trohon, and a double by Jason Farrell helped AllNighters extend their early lead in the second. Mike (The OG) Russo earned the win for AllNighters. The starting pitcher gave up seven hits and five runs over seven innings striking out none and walking none. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Patrick Hosman, McCarthy, Eric (E) Moody, and Trohon each collected two hits. Zametjis led AllNighters with three runs batted in going 1-for-3 on the day.
